entries.section: Leveling Rotation for Shadow Priest DPS in Burning Crusade
entries.text: Engage with . Cast this whenever it's off cooldown.

entries.section: Leveling Rotation for Shadow Priest DPS in Burning Crusade
entries.text: Apply . You can use GCD downtime to move away from the mob.

entries.section: Leveling Rotation for Shadow Priest DPS in Burning Crusade
entries.text: Apply Vampiric Embrace as needed, prioritize it when health starts dropping from safe values. If previous points are executed correctly, the first tick of both DoT effects will be affected.

entries.section: Leveling Rotation for Shadow Priest DPS in Burning Crusade
entries.text: Channel . This has a shorter range than others, but it slows down the enemy a little. You can get in a cast or two before getting pushback. Refresh DoTs if they fall off, but standard mobs should die before then.

entries.section: Leveling Rotation for Shadow Priest DPS in Burning Crusade
entries.text: Pre-shield yourself with before pulling. and Prayer of Mending may also be used, but require reapplying before pulling the first mob.

entries.section: Leveling Rotation for Shadow Priest DPS in Burning Crusade
entries.text: Apply . Engage the target from the maximum range you can.

entries.section: Leveling Rotation for Shadow Priest DPS in Burning Crusade
entries.text: Apply . Use GCD downtime to gain distance towards another mob.

entries.section: Leveling Rotation for Shadow Priest DPS in Burning Crusade
entries.text: Repeat until a satisfactory number of mobs is engaged. 3 is recommended for beginners, but experienced players can manage even 10 under the right circumstances!

entries.section: Leveling Rotation for Shadow Priest DPS in Burning Crusade
entries.text: Damage received from numerous mobs can be dangerous, so use to gain some reprieve from the onslaught.

sections.heading: Leveling Rotation for Shadow Priest DPS in Burning Crusade

entries.section: Leveling Tips for Shadow Priest DPS in TBC Classic
entries.text: Depending on your mana levels, you can rush another mob or wait for a while. grants mana while casting, but 5 seconds after your last successful cast, the amount regenerated is doubled. Manage this mechanic to squeeze as much mana as needed!

entries.section: Leveling Tips for Shadow Priest DPS in TBC Classic
entries.text: You may use the global cooldown downtime of instant spells to strafe away from the mob a little. This delays the mob catching up with you, granting an additional second or two of casting without pushback. Make sure to use strafe movement (running to the sides, which can be keybound in the keybinds menu) and never walk backwards, as it is much slower.

entries.section: Leveling Tips for Shadow Priest DPS in TBC Classic
entries.text: Mass DoT pulls are a viable strategy, but always make sure it is worth the risk. You become a vulnerable gank target on PvP servers. Watch out for respawns and hidden mobs. Try to avoid casters unless you can make good use of .

entries.section: Leveling Tips for Shadow Priest DPS in TBC Classic
entries.text: Spirit is generally considered a useless stat, but this is not the case for Priests. Spirit Tap scales with Spirit and is a major source of regeneration, making the stat definitely worth stacking.

entries.section: Leveling Tips for Shadow Priest DPS in TBC Classic
entries.text: Always keep your buffs up. Even if appears unassuming, it prevents some incoming damage and is not consumed when shielded. now belongs to the Discipline category, making it usable in . Moreover, it can be accessed by all races, so make sure to make good use of this spell, especially on heavily contested PvP servers.
